demographics.living_situation: lives at home with spouse; spouse is present and helping make the call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: There is an elephant sitting on my chest, it's crushing me, my left arm is completely numb, and I feel like I am going to die.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The crushing chest pain came on suddenly about 45 minutes ago while he was sitting watching TV and it has not let up — his spouse is with him and they are frightened.

symptom_narrative.onset: came on suddenly about 45 minutes ago while sitting watching television, doing nothing strenuous
symptom_narrative.duration: about 45 minutes and constant — it has not eased at all
symptom_narrative.character: a heavy crushing, gripping pressure in the middle of the chest, like a huge weight or an elephant sitting on it
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst pain he has ever had; he says he feels like he is going to die
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: middle of the chest, spreading down the inside of the left arm which has gone numb, and up into the jaw
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started, not coming and going
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — resting and changing position have not helped at all
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it is just there all the time regardless of what he does
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: drenched in sweat, feels sick and has vomited once, very short of breath, completely wiped out and exhausted, and an overwhelming feeling that something terrible is about to happen
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this before; he has never had chest trouble
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: completely floored by the pain — cannot move or do anything, just clutching his chest

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: at home with his spouse, who is present and assisting during the call

objective_data_offered.value: he has gone grey and ashen and is drenched in sweat, clutching the centre of his chest and looking terrified
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: appearance described over video/phone by a distressed spouse, not a clinical examination

objective_data_offered.value: he was sick and vomited once, there is a bowl next to him
he is breathing fast and hard and says he can't get enough air
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: lay observation by spouse; not a measured respiratory rate or oxygen level

objective_data_offered.value: he cannot move and is completely still because of the pain and exhaustion
